babyDevelopment: The baby's heart beats at 105-110 bpm. Facial features—jaw, cheeks, chin, ear canals, and eyes—are beginning to form.
bodyChanges: Nausea and fatigue may hit their peak. Your breasts may feel heavy and tingly. Food aversions are common.

do: Eat before getting out of bed
Brush teeth later if toothpaste causes gagging
dont: Worry too much if you can't eat perfectly

travelAdvisory.note: Safe, but expect severe nausea. Pack motion sickness bags and anti-nausea meds.

milestoneDescription: The tiny heart is beating rapidly, pumping blood through the embryo.
